pulse: The Real Estate sector currently trades at a median EV/EBITDA of 20.4x and a median P/E of 40.0x, which are slightly above broader market averages, reflecting continued investor appetite amid rising interest rates. Within this context, Prologis (PLD) has a notable EV/EBITDA of 26.6x and a P/E of 40.0x, which aligns with bullish sentiments indicated by recent headlines like “Here’s what to expect for commercial real estate in 2026,” suggesting strong demand for logistics and industrial spaces. Investors should consider the implications of the robust revenue growth rate of 13.2% reported by Simon Property Group (SPG), as it highlights potential resilience in retail real estate against economic headwinds, in line with observations from Goldman Sachs about finding a firmer footing in the sector.
